page: Amazon Web Services g6.xlarge is a graphics-intensive virtual server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz, configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, a local 250 GB NVMe SSD, and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Hardware acceleration is provided by a single NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU featuring 22 GB of VRAM.

Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct capability profile. The instance delivers top-tier performance in LLM inference tasks, specifically for prompt processing and text generation using both small and medium models. Single-core CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25% of tested systems. However, multi-core CPU performance, Passmark CPU scores, and Geekbench single-core results are average. Memory bandwidth and multi-threaded decompression benchmarks are weak, placing in the bottom 25%.

From a resource density perspective, the g6.xlarge offers a cost-effective entry point for GPU-accelerated workloads due to its bundled NVIDIA L4 GPU and local NVMe storage. The primary tradeoff is the lower memory bandwidth and weaker multi-core scaling, which limits its effectiveness for memory-bound or highly parallel CPU-only tasks. This instance is highly suited for small-to-medium LLM inference, graphics-intensive applications, and lightweight machine learning workloads, rather than heavy database operations or high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services g6.xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz, 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a 250 GB local NVMe SSD. It is equipped with a single NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM, operating on the Nitro hypervisor with a 2 Gbps baseline network speed. Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in LLM inference for small and medium models, alongside strong single-core CPU performance. However, memory bandwidth and multi-core CPU scaling are relatively weak. This hardware profile makes the g6.xlarge highly cost-efficient for GPU-accelerated workloads such as machine learning inference and graphics processing, while being less optimal for memory-intensive database operations or high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6.xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring an AMD EPYC CPU, 16 GB RAM, a 250 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU with 22 GB VRAM for efficient LLM inference.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6.xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU, optimized for machine learning inference.
tagline: An entry-level GPU instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and NVIDIA Ada Lovelace acceleration for efficient model inference.
bullet_points: NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU with 22 GB VRAM
4 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 at 3.4 GHz
16 GB DDR4 memory and 250 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier benchmark performance for small and medium LLM inference
Strong single-core CPU performance with average multi-core scaling
Best suited for machine learning inference and graphics-intensive tasks
